# UC-10.13.1 · Failed Authentication Ratio Trending

## Description

Rising failure ratio vs. successes indicates credential attacks, misconfigured apps, or account lockout waves — normalized via CIM across vendors.

## Value

Rising failure ratio vs. successes indicates credential attacks, misconfigured apps, or account lockout waves — normalized via CIM across vendors.

## Implementation

Accelerate the Authentication data model; alert when `fail_ratio` exceeds baseline + 3σ for the same hour-of-week.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Any TA mapping to `Authentication`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype` values tagged for CIM Authentication (e.g. `WinEventLog:Security`, `o365:audit`, `vpn:syslog`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Accelerate the Authentication data model; alert when `fail_ratio` exceeds baseline + 3σ for the same hour-of-week.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=authentication action=failure OR action=success
| timechart span=1h count(eval(action="failure")) as fail count(eval(action="success")) as ok
| eval fail_ratio=round(fail/(fail+ok)*100,2)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Failed Authentication Ratio Trending** — Rising failure ratio vs. successes indicates credential attacks, misconfigured apps, or account lockout waves — normalized via CIM across vendors.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype` values tagged for CIM Authentication (e.g. `WinEventLog:Security`, `o365:audit`, `vpn:syslog`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Any TA mapping to `Authentication`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **fail_ratio** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, _time span=1h
| xyseries _time Authentication.action count
| fillnull value=0 failure success
| eval fail_ratio=if(failure+success>0, round(failure/(failure+success)*100,2), null())
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Failed Authentication Ratio Trending** — Rising failure ratio vs. successes indicates credential attacks, misconfigured apps, or account lockout waves — normalized via CIM across vendors.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype` values tagged for CIM Authentication (e.g. `WinEventLog:Security`, `o365:audit`, `vpn:syslog`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Any TA mapping to `Authentication`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Pivots fields for charting with `xyseries`.
• Fills null values with `fillnull`.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **fail_ratio** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Accelerate the CIM data model used in the optional tstats path, then run both the primary and `cimSpl` searches over a week that includes a known good baseline. Spot-check a few rows and tune thresholds; confirm no permission or tagging gap is hiding part of the population.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (fail vs. ok), Single value (fail ratio %).

## SPL

```spl
index=security tag=authentication action=failure OR action=success
| timechart span=1h count(eval(action="failure")) as fail count(eval(action="success")) as ok
| eval fail_ratio=round(fail/(fail+ok)*100,2)
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, _time span=1h
| xyseries _time Authentication.action count
| fillnull value=0 failure success
| eval fail_ratio=if(failure+success>0, round(failure/(failure+success)*100,2), null())
```

## Visualization

Line chart (fail vs. ok), Single value (fail ratio %).
